title: Analyzer conformance fixture manifest
description: Language-neutral contract for one captured (or synthetic) analyzer message used as a conformance fixture. The simulator harness and any future driver consume the same fixtures; the manifest carries provenance for ISO 15189 traceability.

id.description: Stable, kebab-case fixture id, unique within the fixtures tree.

description.description: Human-readable summary of what this message exercises.

protocol.description: Application protocol of the captured message.

transport.description: Transport the real instrument speaks; the harness replays via a matching Transport (loopback in the skeleton).

path.description: Raw application-payload bytes file, relative to this manifest. Wire framing is NOT stored here — the transport applies it at replay time.

framing.description: Wire framing the transport must apply/strip around the payload.

reference.description: Provenance: instrument manual section, bench-capture session id, or synthetic-seed note (ISO 15189 evidence chain).

synthetic.description: true = not a real instrument capture (illustrative/skeleton seed). Real conformance captures set false.

channel.description: Per-analyzer channel configuration data. This captures the physical/listener settings required to run the fixture without baking them into parser code.

isolation_group.description: Independent channel/driver group this analyzer belongs to; faults in one group must not require parser or core changes for another.

provenance.description: Where the line-setting values came from. 'bridge-default-bench-pending' marks unconfirmed defaults that must be replaced by a bench capture or vendor doc before go-live.

terminology.description: Per-analyzer vendor-code and unit mapping data used by the simulator/bridge analogs.

expected.description: Placeholder for downstream parser/normalization assertions (e.g. the normalized LOINC/UCUM Result row). Wired up once those services land in Stage 1.
